methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Marathon Fusion is a seed-stage nuclear energy company founded by Adam Rutkowski and Kyle Schiller that has raised $10.9M to date. The nuclear energy sector contains 11 comparable companies: three public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and eight private peers including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Given Marathon's seed stage and early funding position, peer-median valuation proved inapplicable due to insufficient clustering—while eight nuclear energy peers exist, none yielded three or more matches within the same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier. The analysis therefore relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $11M total raised as a proxy valuation baseline. This method implies a post-money valuation of $27M.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $23M to $31M, with a median of $27M. This range reflects the 2.5x step-up typical for seed-to-next-stage progression. Key uncertainties include the absence of directly comparable seed-stage nuclear peers for benchmarking, the applicability of stage-progression multiples to capital-intensive frontier-tech ventures, and the company's specific technology pathway and competitive positioning within the broader nuclear sector. A fuller valuation would benefit from additional peer clustering or revenue/technical milestone data.
